calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Bryan'S Market, Inc.'S Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3600.00
Off Calendar Mar-11-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Conference:
Oliver Dreger, Esq., of GODES & PREIS, LLP., Tel:949.468.0051 for plaintiffs
Susanna L. Chenette, Esq., of HANSON BRIDGETT LLP. Tel: 415.777.3200 for defendants
Court Reporter: Mary Argyropoulos CSR # 9775, Tel: 9253105515, Email: argyreporting@gmail.com.

Hearing held: A untimely Notice of Contest was received from defendant's counsel. At 4:01 p.m., Defendant's counsel sent an email to the Court without copying the other parties. Two minutes later, at 4:03 p.m., Defendant's counsel sent a second email that included the other parties. Subsequently, the Court clerk advised that the proposed order should be submitted verbatim in accordance with the Court's tentative ruling. Plaintiff's counsel, as the prevailing party, submitted the proposed order to the Court at 9:15 a.m. The proposed order reflected the substantive substance of the Court's tentative ruling only. Despite follow-up communications and emails reiterating that the proposed order must track the tentative ruling verbatim. As a one-time courtesy, the Court will hear the matter. In the future, if the parties fail to follow the Court's instructions, the Court will not hear the matter. The Court having heard oral argument simultaneously the 2 motions and having reviewed the pleadings the Court adopts its tentative ruling.

1 - Petitioners Petra Flannery and Meehan Flannery O'Neill's Motion To Compel Defendant Bryan's Market, Inc.'s Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3600.00 is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.

(see word minutes for full details)

(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Macadangdang, Court Reporter: Mary Argyropoulos CSR # 9775, Tel: 9253105515, Email: argyreporting@gmail.com. Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant P&T Flannery Seafoods, Inc.'S Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3600.00
Off Calendar Feb-19-2026 Continued To Mar-11-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER PETRA FLANNERY , CO-TRUSTEE OF THE FLANNERY FAMILY LIVING TRUST, MEEHAN O'NEILL Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant P&T Flannery Seafoods, Inc.'S Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3600.00

Appearances (by Zoom):
Oliver Dreger, Esq. 949-468-0051 Godes & Preis for Plaintiff.
Susanna L. Chennette, Esq. 415-777-3200 Hanson Bridgett LLP for Defendant.
Court reporter: Trina Ayala, CSR #12964, 650-267-1302, tayalacsr@gmail.com

Hearing is held. Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling is hearing required and makes a disclosure re: being a partner at Hanson Bridgett before joining the bench. Mr. Dreger inquires re duration of time. The Court responds. No further questions from Mr. Dreger. The Court states He has not read the papers and will continue the hearing to March 11, 2026 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 302. Ms. Chenette inquires re: a supplemental briefing. The Court states briefing is closed. Both parites waive notice. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

After discussion, The Court rules as follows:

2 - PETITIONERs PETRA FLANNERY, and MEEHAN O'NEILL's Motion To Compel Defendant P&T Flannery Seafoods, Inc.'S Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3600.00.

Continued to March 11, 2026 at 9:00am in Dept. 302.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Trina Ayala, CSR #12964, 650-267-1302, tayalacsr@gmail.com, Reported. (302/JMQ)

The Court states He has not read the papers and will continue the hearing to March 11, 2026 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 302. Ms. Chenette inquires re: a supplemental briefing. The Court states briefing is closed. Both parites waive notice. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

After discussion, The Court rules as follows:

1 - PETITIONERs PETRA FLANNERY, and MEEHAN O'NEILL's Motion To Compel Defendant Bryan'S Market, Inc.'S Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One;Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3600.00.

Continued to March 11, 2026 at 9:00am in Dept. 302.
